references.notes: The verse refers to 'al-Dahr' (Time/Fate) as an active agent that 'snatched away', reflecting the pre-Islamic cosmological concept of Dahr as an inescapable force.
The verse mentions 'al-Manāyā' (fates/deaths) as an ever-present 'ambush', indicating the pre-Islamic belief in inescapable destiny.
The verse expresses a wish to be offered to 'al-Manāyā' (fates/deaths) in place of the deceased, highlighting the pre-Islamic concept of fate as an ultimate arbiter of life and death.
